title: euphorbia graeca subsp. dalmatica
intro: Euphorbia graeca subsp. dalmatica, also known as Dalmatian spurge, is a subspecies of the Euphorbia graeca plant, native to the Dalmatian region of Croatia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and purgative properties, due to the presence of diterpenes and other bioactive compounds. Euphorbia graeca subsp. dalmatica has been found to have various health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, fever reduction, and antiseptic properties, making it a useful remedy for wounds and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, tinctures, and ointments, and can also be dried and used as a tea or added to topical creams and salves.
